title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: Search usually first step. Results include O*NET-SOC code occupation title.
code: software_jobs <- if (run_live) {   onet_search("software developer", start = 1, end = 5) } else {   tibble(     code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1251.00", "15-2051.00"),     title = c(       "Software Developers",       "Computer Programmers",       "Data Scientists"     )   ) }  head(software_jobs, 5) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   code       title                #>   <chr>      <chr>                #> 1 15-1252.00 Software Developers  #> 2 15-1251.00 Computer Programmers #> 3 15-2051.00 Data Scientists

title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: detail endpoints return tibbles. makes easy inspect, join, summarize. pattern works knowledge, abilities, tasks, work activities, work styles, work context, interests, technology, education, job zones.
code: skills <- if (run_live) {   onet_skills("15-1252.00", start = 1, end = 5) } else {   tibble(     id = c("2.A.1.a", "2.A.1.b", "2.B.3.m"),     name = c(       "Reading Comprehension",       "Active Listening",       "Programming"     ),     description = c(       "Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work-related documents.",       "Giving full attention to what other people are saying.",       "Writing computer programs for various purposes."     ),     value = c(4.12, 4.00, 4.38),     scale = c("Importance", "Importance", "Importance")   ) }  head(skills, 5) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   id      name                  description                          value scale #>   <chr>   <chr>                 <chr>                                <dbl> <chr> #> 1 2.A.1.a Reading Comprehension Understanding written sentences and…  4.12 Impo… #> 2 2.A.1.b Active Listening      Giving full attention to what other…  4    Impo… #> 3 2.B.3.m Programming           Writing computer programs for vario…  4.38 Impo…

title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: O*NET database tables expose lower-level source data. Use onet_tables() find available tables onet_table_info() inspect columns downloading full table.
code: tables <- if (run_live) {   onet_tables() } else {   tibble(     id = c("Occupation Data", "Abilities", "Skills", "Task Ratings"),     title = c("Occupation Data", "Abilities", "Skills", "Task Ratings")   ) }  head(tables, 6) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   id              title           #>   <chr>           <chr>           #> 1 Occupation Data Occupation Data #> 2 Abilities       Abilities       #> 3 Skills          Skills          #> 4 Task Ratings    Task Ratings table_info <- if (run_live) {   onet_table_info("Abilities") } else {   tibble(     name = c("onetsoc_code", "element_id", "scale_id", "data_value"),     title = c("O*NET-SOC Code", "Element ID", "Scale ID", "Data Value"),     type = c("character", "character", "character", "decimal"),     format = c(NA, NA, NA, "n.n")   ) }  head(table_info, 8) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   name         title          type      format #>   <chr>        <chr>          <chr>     <chr>  #> 1 onetsoc_code O*NET-SOC Code character NA     #> 2 element_id   Element ID     character NA     #> 3 scale_id     Scale ID       character NA     #> 4 data_value   Data Value     decimal   n.n

title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: O*NET describes occupations . BLS OEWS adds employment wage scale. example uses package’s bundled OEWS sample always runs.
code: sample_oews <- system.file(   "extdata",   "oews-national-sample.csv",   package = "onet2r" )  oews <- onet_oews_national(2024, path = sample_oews)  occupations <- tibble(   code = c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "11-1011.00"),   title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses", "Chief Executives") )  occupation_wages <- onet_join_oews(occupations, oews = oews) head(occupation_wages, 5) #> # A tibble: 3 × 9 #>   code       title  soc_code  year oews_type occ_title tot_emp a_median h_median #>   <chr>      <chr>  <chr>    <int> <chr>     <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 15-1252.00 Softw… 15-1252   2024 national  Software… 1847900   133080     64.0 #> 2 29-1141.00 Regis… 29-1141   2024 national  Register… 3175400    93070     44.8 #> 3 11-1011.00 Chief… 11-1011   2024 national  Chief Ex…  211230   206680     99.4

title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: Employment-weighted summaries useful descriptor appears across occupations different labor-market sizes.
code: skill_rows <- tibble(   code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "29-1141.00"),   element_id = c("2.A.1.a", "2.A.1.b", "2.A.1.a", "2.A.1.b"),   element_name = c(     "Reading Comprehension",     "Active Listening",     "Reading Comprehension",     "Active Listening"   ),   data_value = c(4.12, 4.00, 3.88, 4.25) )  weighted_skills <- onet_weighted_summary(   skill_rows,   group = c("element_id", "element_name"),   value = "data_value",   oews = oews )  head(weighted_skills, 5) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   element_id element_name     n_records n_occupations total_weight weighted_mean #>   <chr>      <chr>                <int>         <int>        <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 2.A.1.a    Reading Compreh…         2             2      5023300          3.97 #> 2 2.A.1.b    Active Listening         2             2      5023300          4.16 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: wage_weighted_mean <dbl>

title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: API serves current release. compare releases time, use archive helpers. See longitudinal articles methodological cautions.
code: panel <- tibble(   release_version = rep(c("1.0", "2.0"), each = 2),   release_date = rep(as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01")), each = 2),   soc_vintage = "2019",   domain = "Abilities",   onet_soc_code = rep(c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"), 2),   soc_code = rep(c("15-1252", "29-1141"), 2),   title = rep(c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses"), 2),   element_id = "1.A.1.a.1",   element_name = "Oral Comprehension",   scale_id = "IM",   data_value = c(4.0, 4.2, 4.4, 4.2),   source_date = as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2020-01-01")),   domain_source = "Analyst" )  bridge <- tibble(   from_vintage = "2019",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141"),   to_soc_code = c("15-1252", "29-1141"),   from_title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses"),   to_title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses"),   step_count = 0L,   map_type = "one_to_one",   crosswalk_weight = 1 )  changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(panel, bridge) head(changes, 5) #> # A tibble: 2 × 26 #>   from_release to_release from_release_date to_release_date from_soc_code #>   <chr>        <chr>      <date>            <date>          <chr>         #> 1 1.0          2.0        2020-01-01        2021-01-01      15-1252       #> 2 1.0          2.0        2020-01-01        2021-01-01      29-1141       #> # ℹ 21 more variables: to_soc_code <chr>, soc_vintage_from <chr>, #> #   soc_vintage_to <chr>, domain <chr>, element_id <chr>, element_name <chr>, #> #   scale_id <chr>, from_value <dbl>, to_value <dbl>, value_change <dbl>, #> #   value_percent_change <dbl>, from_source_date <date>, to_source_date <date>, #> #   date_changed <lgl>, value_changed <lgl>, change_type <fct>, #> #   method_break <lgl>, crosswalk_uncertain <lgl>, safely_comparable <lgl>, #> #   map_type <chr>, crosswalk_weight <dbl>

title: Getting Started with onet2r
text: Read vignette("longitudinal-onet-background", package = "onet2r") interpreting cross-release changes. Read vignette("longitudinal-archives", package = "onet2r") assemble reconcile archive panels. Read vignette("oews-wage-context", package = "onet2r") add BLS wage employment estimates.

title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: Live release discovery reads public O*NET Resource Center archive page. package function returns one row per downloadable text archive. example uses small representative table article builds without network access. interactive workflow, call onet_releases() choose versions want compare.
code: releases <- tibble(   version = c("30.3", "30.2", "30.1"),   release_date = as.Date(c("2026-05-01", "2026-02-01", "2025-11-01")),   year = c(2026L, 2026L, 2025L),   month = c("May", "February", "November"),   soc_vintage = c("2019", "2019", "2019"),   text_url = c(     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_3_text.zip",     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_2_text.zip",     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_1_text.zip"   ),   dictionary_url = c(     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dictionary/30.3/excel/",     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dictionary/30.2/excel/",     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dictionary/30.1/excel/"   ) )  head(releases, 3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   version release_date  year month    soc_vintage text_url        dictionary_url #>   <chr>   <date>       <int> <chr>    <chr>       <chr>           <chr>          #> 1 30.3    2026-05-01    2026 May      2019        https://www.on… https://www.o… #> 2 30.2    2026-02-01    2026 February 2019        https://www.on… https://www.o… #> 3 30.1    2025-11-01    2025 November 2019        https://www.on… https://www.o…

title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: onet_archive_read() normalizes descriptor-like archive tables long schema. onet_panel() row-binds table across releases. example uses tiny panel columns. live data, use pattern archive helpers: onet_panel("Abilities", versions = c("30.2", "30.3"), scale = "IM").
code: panel <- tibble(   release_version = rep(c("1.0", "2.0"), each = 4),   release_date = rep(as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01")), each = 4),   soc_vintage = "2019",   domain = "Abilities",   onet_soc_code = rep(     c("11-1011.00", "15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "41-1011.00"),     2   ),   soc_code = rep(c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"), 2),   title = "Occupation",   element_id = "1.A.1.a.1",   element_name = "Oral Comprehension",   scale_id = "IM",   data_value = c(4, 4, 4, 4, 4, 5, 4, 5),   source_date = c(     as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01", "2020-01-01")),     as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2021-01-01", "2020-01-01"))   ),   domain_source = "Analyst" )  head(panel, 6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 13 #>   release_version release_date soc_vintage domain   onet_soc_code soc_code title #>   <chr>           <date>       <chr>       <chr>    <chr>         <chr>    <chr> #> 1 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 11-1011.00    11-1011  Occu… #> 2 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 15-1252.00    15-1252  Occu… #> 3 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 29-1141.00    29-1141  Occu… #> 4 1.0             2020-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 41-1011.00    41-1011  Occu… #> 5 2.0             2021-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 11-1011.00    11-1011  Occu… #> 6 2.0             2021-01-01   2019        Abiliti… 15-1252.00    15-1252  Occu… #> # ℹ 6 more variables: element_id <chr>, element_name <chr>, scale_id <chr>, #> #   data_value <dbl>, source_date <date>, domain_source <chr>

title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: releases use SOC vintage, bridge one--one. Across vintages, onet_crosswalk_bridge() composes official adjacent crosswalks labels split merge rows.
code: bridge <- tibble(   from_vintage = "2019",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_soc_code = c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"),   to_soc_code = c("11-1011", "15-1252", "29-1141", "41-1011"),   from_title = "Occupation",   to_title = "Occupation",   step_count = 0L,   map_type = "one_to_one",   crosswalk_weight = 1 )  head(bridge, 6) #> # A tibble: 4 × 9 #>   from_vintage to_vintage from_soc_code to_soc_code from_title to_title   #>   <chr>        <chr>      <chr>         <chr>       <chr>      <chr>      #> 1 2019         2019       11-1011       11-1011     Occupation Occupation #> 2 2019         2019       15-1252       15-1252     Occupation Occupation #> 3 2019         2019       29-1141       29-1141     Occupation Occupation #> 4 2019         2019       41-1011       41-1011     Occupation Occupation #> # ℹ 3 more variables: step_count <int>, map_type <chr>, crosswalk_weight <dbl>

title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: onet_panel_reconcile() compares adjacent releases classifies matched occupation-element-scale pair. change classification : method_break crosswalk_uncertain overrides. row safely comparable truth-table result comparable neither override present.
code: changes <- onet_panel_reconcile(panel, bridge)  head(changes[, c(   "from_soc_code",   "to_soc_code",   "from_value",   "to_value",   "value_change",   "date_changed",   "change_type",   "safely_comparable" )], 8) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   from_soc_code to_soc_code from_value to_value value_change date_changed #>   <chr>         <chr>            <dbl>    <dbl>        <dbl> <lgl>        #> 1 11-1011       11-1011              4        4            0 FALSE        #> 2 15-1252       15-1252              4        5            1 TRUE         #> 3 29-1141       29-1141              4        4            0 TRUE         #> 4 41-1011       41-1011              4        5            1 FALSE        #> # ℹ 2 more variables: change_type <fct>, safely_comparable <lgl>

title: Longitudinal O*NET Archive Analysis
text: Pick domain table Abilities, Skills, Work Activities. Use onet_releases() identify archive versions SOC vintages. Build panel onet_panel(). Build bridge onet_crosswalk_bridge(). Reconcile onet_panel_reconcile(). Inspect method_break, crosswalk_uncertain, safely_comparable drawing substantive conclusions.

title: Why Longitudinal O*NET Analysis Is Hard
text: API workflows ask “occupation look like now?” Longitudinal work asks “changed release release B?” different questions. panels, use archive functions.
code: release_example <- tibble(   version = c("30.3", "30.2", "30.1"),   release_date = as.Date(c("2026-05-01", "2026-02-01", "2025-11-01")),   soc_vintage = c("2019", "2019", "2019"),   text_url = c(     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_3_text.zip",     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_2_text.zip",     "https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database/db_30_1_text.zip"   ) )  head(release_example, 3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   version release_date soc_vintage text_url                                      #>   <chr>   <date>       <chr>       <chr>                                         #> 1 30.3    2026-05-01   2019        https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database… #> 2 30.2    2026-02-01   2019        https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database… #> 3 30.1    2025-11-01   2019        https://www.onetcenter.org/dl_files/database…

title: Why Longitudinal O*NET Analysis Is Hard
text: ONET-SOC vintages line one--one forever. Occupations split, merge, appear, disappear. Hosseinioun et al. (2025) handle composing adjacent official ONET-SOC crosswalks, strategy used onet_crosswalk_bridge(). Rows marked split merge thrown away. flagged crosswalk-uncertain reconciliation users can decide whether keep, weight, exclude .
code: bridge_example <- tibble(   from_vintage = "2010",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_soc_code = c("15-1132", "15-1132", "29-1141"),   to_soc_code = c("15-1252", "15-1253", "29-1141"),   map_type = c("split", "split", "one_to_one"),   crosswalk_weight = c(0.5, 0.5, 1) )  head(bridge_example, 3) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   from_vintage to_vintage from_soc_code to_soc_code map_type   crosswalk_weight #>   <chr>        <chr>      <chr>         <chr>       <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1 2010         2019       15-1132       15-1252     split                   0.5 #> 2 2010         2019       15-1132       15-1253     split                   0.5 #> 3 2010         2019       29-1141       29-1141     one_to_one              1

what: 3. A Release-to-Release “Change” May Not Be a True Content Change
title: Why Longitudinal O*NET Analysis Is Hard
text: Many O*NET archive tables include source date domain source. fields matter value can appear unchanged simply occupation re-surveyed, value can change without source-date change recode recalculation. Handel (2016) useful entry point broader measurement comparability cautions.
code: tibble(   value_changed = c(FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE),   source_date_changed = c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE),   change_type = c(     "stale_carryforward",     "real_update",     "resampled_stable",     "recode_or_recalc_flag"   ),   comparable_before_overrides = c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE) ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   value_changed source_date_changed change_type           comparable_before_ov…¹ #>   <lgl>         <lgl>               <chr>                 <lgl>                  #> 1 FALSE         FALSE               stale_carryforward    TRUE                   #> 2 TRUE          TRUE                real_update           TRUE                   #> 3 FALSE         TRUE                resampled_stable      TRUE                   #> 4 TRUE          FALSE               recode_or_recalc_flag FALSE                  #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​comparable_before_overrides

what: How This Differs from the Standard Task Approach
title: Why Longitudinal O*NET Analysis Is Hard
text: canonical task-framework literature often avoids ONET’s version--version variation. Instead, holds task scores fixed gets time variation changing occupational employment shares (Autor, Levy, Murnane 2003; Autor Dorn 2013; Autor 2013). approach standard cleaner many labor-market questions. onet2r enables complementary route: directly comparing archived ONET content marking taxonomy survey-timing problems. Direct within-occupation change work closer longitudinal module . Consoli et al. (2023) combine DOT ONET study within-occupation task change long period. Chen et al. (2026) report narrower ONET version comparison one stylized fact inside paper whose main data source online vacancies.

title: Why Longitudinal O*NET Analysis Is Hard
text: O*NET Database Releases Archive source release ZIP files (National Center O*NET Development, n.d.b). O*NET Data Dictionary documents table structure, date fields, source fields, historical content changes (National Center O*NET Development, n.d.). O*NET-SOC taxonomy files provide official adjacent crosswalks used bridge layer (National Center O*NET Development, n.d.c). BLS provides methodological overview mapping Employment Projections O*NET data, including split, merge, imputation issues motivate future employment-weighted bridge options (U.S. Bureau Labor Statistics 2021).

what: Join OEWS to O*NET occupation codes
title: Adding OEWS wage and employment context
text: O*NET-SOC codes commonly include detail suffix, .00. OEWS uses six-digit SOC codes. onet_join_oews() creates soc_code column joins normalized code.
code: occupations <- tibble::tibble(   code = c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "11-1011.00"),   title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses", "Chief Executives") )  occupation_wages <- onet_join_oews(occupations, oews = oews) occupation_wages #> # A tibble: 3 × 9 #>   code       title  soc_code  year oews_type occ_title tot_emp a_median h_median #>   <chr>      <chr>  <chr>    <int> <chr>     <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 15-1252.00 Softw… 15-1252   2024 national  Software… 1847900   133080     64.0 #> 2 29-1141.00 Regis… 29-1141   2024 national  Register… 3175400    93070     44.8 #> 3 11-1011.00 Chief… 11-1011   2024 national  Chief Ex…  211230   206680     99.4

title: Adding OEWS wage and employment context
text: Many research workflows need turn occupation-level O*NET records labor-market-weighted measures. example, skill can appear multiple occupations different skill ratings different employment levels. output includes: n_records: number rows used grouped summary n_occupations: number occupations contributing summary total_weight: sum employment weights weighted_mean: employment-weighted mean selected O*NET value wage_weighted_mean: mean weighted employment multiplied annual median wage
code: skill_rows <- tibble::tibble(   code = c("15-1252.00", "15-1252.00", "29-1141.00", "29-1141.00"),   element_id = c("2.A.1.a", "2.A.1.b", "2.A.1.a", "2.A.1.b"),   element_name = c(     "Reading Comprehension",     "Active Listening",     "Reading Comprehension",     "Active Listening"   ),   data_value = c(4.12, 4.00, 3.88, 4.25) )  onet_weighted_summary(   skill_rows,   group = c("element_id", "element_name"),   value = "data_value",   oews = oews ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   element_id element_name     n_records n_occupations total_weight weighted_mean #>   <chr>      <chr>                <int>         <int>        <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 2.A.1.a    Reading Compreh…         2             2      5023300          3.97 #> 2 2.A.1.b    Active Listening         2             2      5023300          4.16 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: wage_weighted_mean <dbl>

title: Adding OEWS wage and employment context
text: workflow uses ACS CPS microdata instead OEWS employment counts, onet_pums_employment_weights() converts individual records SOC-level weights. SOCP PWGTP names match common ACS PUMS output tidycensus::get_pums(). live ACS pulls, start tidycensus::get_pums() pass resulting data frame onet_pums_employment_weights(). site reports whether tidycensus installed, keeps example local reproducible.
code: pums <- tibble::tibble(   SOCP = c("151252", "151252", "291141"),   PWGTP = c(120, 80, 200) )  onet_pums_employment_weights(pums) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   soc_code employment records #>   <chr>         <dbl>   <int> #> 1 15-1252         200       2 #> 2 29-1141         200       1 tibble::tibble(   optional_package = "tidycensus",   installed = requireNamespace("tidycensus", quietly = TRUE) ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   optional_package installed #>   <chr>            <lgl>     #> 1 tidycensus       FALSE

title: Validating onet2r outputs
text: validation script always checks deterministic local outputs: reading bundled OEWS sample data joining O*NET occupation codes OEWS estimates computing employment- wage-weighted O*NET summaries configuring cache rate-limit settings ONET_API_KEY available, also performs live smoke checks across main exported O*NET functions. result inspected expected type, minimum row count, important columns.

what: Example: Local Validation without an API Key
title: Validating onet2r outputs
code: library(onet2r) library(tibble)  sample_oews <- system.file(   "extdata",   "oews-national-sample.csv",   package = "onet2r" )  oews <- onet_oews_national(2024, path = sample_oews)  validation <- tibble(   check = c(     "OEWS output is a tibble",     "OEWS has wage and employment columns",     "join adds normalized SOC code"   ),   passed = c(     inherits(oews, "tbl_df"),     all(c("occ_code", "tot_emp", "a_median") %in% names(oews)),     TRUE   ) )  occupations <- tibble(   code = c("15-1252.00", "29-1141.00"),   title = c("Software Developers", "Registered Nurses") )  joined <- onet_join_oews(occupations, oews = oews) validation$passed[3] <- all(c("soc_code", "tot_emp", "a_median") %in% names(joined))  validation #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   check                                passed #>   <chr>                                <lgl>  #> 1 OEWS output is a tibble              TRUE   #> 2 OEWS has wage and employment columns TRUE   #> 3 join adds normalized SOC code        TRUE head(joined, 5) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   code       title  soc_code  year oews_type occ_title tot_emp a_median h_median #>   <chr>      <chr>  <chr>    <int> <chr>     <chr>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 15-1252.00 Softw… 15-1252   2024 national  Software… 1847900   133080     64.0 #> 2 29-1141.00 Regis… 29-1141   2024 national  Register… 3175400    93070     44.8

title: Access O*NET Web Services API data from R
text: O*NET API requests require API key. Register free key . Recommended: Store key .Renviron persistence across sessions: Add line: Restart R load environment variable. Alternative: Set current session : Verify key configured:
code: usethis::edit_r_environ() ONET_API_KEY=your-api-key-here Sys.setenv(ONET_API_KEY = "your-api-key-here") library(onet2r) onet_api_key()

title: Access O*NET Web Services API data from R
code: library(onet2r)  # Search for occupations onet_search("software developer")  # List occupations (paged) occ <- onet_occupations(start = 1, end = 25)  # Get occupation summary onet_occupation("15-1252.00")  # Retrieve structured details (returns tibbles) onet_skills("15-1252.00", end = 5) onet_knowledge("15-1252.00", end = 5) onet_abilities("15-1252.00", end = 5) onet_tasks("15-1252.00", end = 5)  # Technology endpoints onet_hot_technology("15-1252.00", end = 5) onet_technology_skills("15-1252.00", end = 2)

title: Access O*NET Web Services API data from R
text: typical workflow: search → select → pull details.
code: library(dplyr) library(onet2r)  # Find target occupation target_code <- onet_search("data scientist") |>   filter(title == "Data Scientists") |>   pull(code)  # Pull structured details skills <- onet_skills(target_code, end = 25) tasks  <- onet_tasks(target_code, end = 25) tech   <- onet_hot_technology(target_code, end = 25)  # Pull complete occupation-level sections for downstream analysis all_occupations <- onet_occupations_all(show_progress = FALSE) all_skills <- onet_skills_all(target_code, show_progress = FALSE)  # Combine skills from multiple occupations codes <- c("15-2051.00", "15-1252.00")  all_skills <- codes |>   purrr::map(\(code) onet_skills_all(code, show_progress = FALSE) |> dplyr::mutate(code = code)) |>   purrr::list_rbind()  # Join BLS OEWS national employment and wage estimates oews <- onet_oews_national(2024) wage_context <- all_occupations |>   onet_join_oews(oews = oews)  # If BLS rate-limits automated downloads, download the ZIP manually from # the BLS OEWS public data pages and pass the local file path: oews <- onet_oews_national(2024, path = "oesm24nat.zip")  # Compute employment-weighted skill summaries weighted_skills <- all_skills |>   onet_weighted_summary(     group = c("element_id", "element_name"),     value = "data_value",     oews = oews   )  # Convert ACS/CPS PUMS records to occupation employment weights pums_weights <- onet_pums_employment_weights(pums, socp = "SOCP", weight = "PWGTP")

title: Reconcile O\*NET Panel Changes — onet_panel_reconcile
code: panel <- tibble::tibble(   release_version = c("29.0", "30.0", "29.0", "30.0"),   release_date = as.Date(c("2024-08-01", "2025-08-01", "2024-08-01", "2025-08-01")),   soc_vintage = rep("2019", 4),   domain = "Abilities",   onet_soc_code = rep("11-1011.00", 4),   soc_code = rep("11-1011", 4),   element_id = c("1.A.1.a.1", "1.A.1.a.1", "1.A.1.a.2", "1.A.1.a.2"),   element_name = c(     "Oral Comprehension", "Oral Comprehension",     "Written Comprehension", "Written Comprehension"   ),   scale_id = rep("IM", 4),   data_value = c(4.5, 4.7, 4.2, 4.2),   source_date = as.Date(c("2023-08-01", "2024-08-01", "2023-08-01", "2023-08-01")),   domain_source = rep("Analyst", 4) ) bridge <- tibble::tibble(   from_vintage = "2019",   to_vintage = "2019",   from_soc_code = "11-1011",   to_soc_code = "11-1011",   map_type = "one_to_one",   crosswalk_weight = 1 ) onet_panel_reconcile(panel, bridge) #> # A tibble: 2 × 26 #>   from_release to_release from_release_date to_release_date from_soc_code #>   <chr>        <chr>      <date>            <date>          <chr>         #> 1 29.0         30.0       2024-08-01        2025-08-01      11-1011       #> 2 29.0         30.0       2024-08-01        2025-08-01      11-1011       #> # ℹ 21 more variables: to_soc_code <chr>, soc_vintage_from <chr>, #> #   soc_vintage_to <chr>, domain <chr>, element_id <chr>, element_name <chr>, #> #   scale_id <chr>, from_value <dbl>, to_value <dbl>, value_change <dbl>, #> #   value_percent_change <dbl>, from_source_date <date>, to_source_date <date>, #> #   date_changed <lgl>, value_changed <lgl>, change_type <fct>, #> #   method_break <lgl>, crosswalk_uncertain <lgl>, safely_comparable <lgl>, #> #   map_type <chr>, crosswalk_weight <dbl>

title: onet2r 0.2.0
text: Added longitudinal O*NET background article verified reference file help users understand archive panels, taxonomy changes, comparability limits interpreting release--release changes (issue). onet_archive_download() downloads text-format O*NET database archives release version reproducible local analysis (issue). onet_archive_read() reads descriptor tables downloaded archives normalized long panel schema (issue). onet_change_summary() summarizes reconciled O*NET changes overall SOC job family (issue). onet_crosswalk_bridge() builds SOC taxonomy bridges across supported O*NET vintages labels one--one, split, merge mappings (issue). onet_panel() assembles descriptor tables across multiple O*NET releases longitudinal analysis (issue). onet_panel_reconcile() classifies release--release changes real updates, stable resampling, stale carryforwards, recode/recalculation flags (issue). onet_releases() lists O*NET database releases, archive URLs, dictionary URLs, release dates, inferred SOC taxonomy vintages (issue).

title: onet2r 0.1.0
text: onet_cache_use(), onet_cache_clear(), onet_rate_limit() add optional local response caching request spacing repeated bulk O*NET API workflows. onet_join_oews() joins O*NET occupation tibbles national BLS Occupational Employment Wage Statistics (OEWS) employment wage estimates. onet_oews(), onet_oews_national(), onet_oews_state(), onet_oews_metro(), onet_oews_industry() download parse BLS OEWS estimates SOC-level wage employment context. onet_pums_employment_weights() converts ACS/CPS-style PUMS occupation records SOC-level employment weights O*NET joins. onet_weighted_summary() computes employment- wage-weighted summaries O*NET task, skill, ability, work-activity rows. Initial development release O*NET authentication, occupation search, occupation detail helpers, database table access, pagination, military crosswalks, taxonomy mapping.
